data.title: Interpublic stock sinks after lackluster Q3 report
China’s consumer confidence continues to gradually slip – MS
data.description:
Morgan Stanley’s AlphaWise Consumer Pulse Survey in late September showed that sentiment for China’s economy remains weak.

The survey encompassed 2,000 consumer respondents and found that confidence in China’s economy slipped, said Katy L. Huberty, the global head of research at Morgan Stanley, in a note.

The survey showed that 49% of consumers expect the Chinese economy to improve in the next six months. But the percentage is down 6 percentage points from 55% in June.

About 13% of respondents anticipate the Chinese economy to get worse, up 1 percentage point from June; and about 38% of respondents said they expect the economy to stay the same, up 5 percentage points from June.

In addition, household spending intentions continued to trend down, the survey showed, with 25% of consumers expecting to spend more in the next quarter, down 3 percentage points quarter-over-quarter. About 10% expect to spend less – 2 percentage points higher quarter-over-quarter.

Early this week, China cut the benchmark lending rates by 25 basis points to 3.10% as a part of a package of stimulus measures to boost its economy. The five-year loan prime rate was also cut 25 basis points to 3.6%, and the one-year loan prime rate was cut to 3.10%.

“Our China economists believe the weakening consumer sentiment points to a need for further consumption stimulus to help alleviate the deflation spiral,” said Huberty.

China ETFs: (NASDAQ:MCHI), (FXI), (GXC).

data.title: Peloton falls as Costco partnership is viewed cautiously
data.description:
Peloton Interactive (NASDAQ:PTON) jumped fell back from an early gain after the company announced a new partnership with Costco (NASDAQ:COST) to sell the Peloton Bike+ online and in 300 of its U.S. stores this holiday season.

Starting November 1, Costco (COST) members will be able to purchase an exclusive Bike+ bundle through February 15, 2025. "The Costco brand is incredibly powerful and respected in the US and abroad. Costco members know they are shopping for high-quality products from trusted brands with the best value," stated Dion Camp Sanders, Chief Emerging Business Officer, Peloton.

The Peloton Bike+, which is self-assembly, is expected to retail for $1,999 in-store and $2,199 online (including delivery), and comes with a 48-month extended warranty. The All-Access Membership, required to access content on the Peloton Bike+ for $44 USD/month, allows members to create accounts for the entire household. The Membership provides unlimited access to Peloton’s content library with 16 modalities from cycling to bike bootcamp, strength, pilates, meditation, and more.

Analysts on Wall Street expressed some concern about the implications of Peloton (PTON) striking another retail deal, which could end up cutting margins.

Shares of Peloton Interactive (PTON) were down 2.4% in Tuesday morning trading as investors viewed the Costco (COST) partnership announcement with some skepticism.

data.title: Akebia stock climbs 8% on dialysis provider supply agreement
data.description:
• Akebia (NASDAQ:AKBA) shares rose 8% Tuesday after the company announced it had signed a multi-year commercial supply contract with a leading dialysis services provider for its recently approved drug Vafseo.
  • Akebia said the contract enables physicians to prescribe Vafseo to dialysis patients when deemed clinically appropriate. Vafseo is expected to be launched in the U.S. in January 2025, according to a statement.
  • The FDA approved Vafseo, also known as vadadustat, in March for the treatment of anemia due to chronic kidney disease in patients who have been receiving dialysis for at least three months.
  • In October, the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services granted Transitional Drug Add-On Payment Adjustment reimbursement for Vafseo, starting on Jan. 1, 2025.

data.title: Stripe's takeover of Bridge 'validates the usage and growth of stablecoins' - Bernstein
data.description:
Stripe's (STRIP) $1.1B acquisition of Bridge validates the usage and growth of stablecoins as a legit use case for public blockchains," Bernstein wrote in a note to clients on Tuesday.

The move comes as Stripe (STRIP) seeks to become "the world's best stablecoin infrastructure," its CEO has said.

Bridge plays "an important role by building API software for businesses to integrate stablecoin payments within their regular payments experience," analyst Gautam Chhugani wrote.

The overall backdrop for stablecoins appears strong. "With improvements in blockchain scalability, stablecoins have emerged as the leading use case for blockchains, particularly for cross-border payments," he noted.

The analyst added that U.S. dollar-denominated stablecoins utilizing crypto rails have emerged as the most cost-effective means of cross-border payments, with costs ranging from just 1 to 2 basis points.

Konstantin Shulga, CEO and co-founder of Finery Markets, thinks the Bridge deal signals the onset of a big wave of consolidation within the crypto space. "The price of the $1.1 billion suggests [Stripe] anticipate[s] rapid stablecoin adoption and want[s] immediate access to the technology that could facilitate this transition."

data.title: NVR GAAP EPS of $130.50 misses by $1.30, revenue of $2.73B beats by $30M
data.description:
• NVR press release (NYSE:NVR): Q3 GAAP EPS of $130.50 misses by $1.30.
  • Revenue of $2.73B (+6.2% Y/Y) beats by $30M.
  • New orders in the third quarter of 2024 increased by 19% to 5,650 units, when compared to 4,746 units in the third quarter of 2023.
  • The average sales price of new orders in the third quarter of 2024 was $450,700, a decrease of 1% when compared with the third quarter of 2023.
  • The cancellation rate in the third quarter of 2024 was 15% compared to 14% in the third quarter of 2023.
  • Settlements in the third quarter of 2024 increased by 5% to 5,908 units, compared to 5,606 units in the third quarter of 2023.

data.title: NextEra Energy Q3 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
NextEra Energy (NYSE:NEE) is set to post third quarter results on Wednesday, before the market opens.

Wall Street expects the U.S. clean energy developer to post EPS of $0.99, implying a 5.3% rise, while revenue is expected to rise 11.7% to $8.01 billion.

The demand for electricity, fuelled by the growth in data centers and AI and the continued expansion of its renewable energy sources portfolio, is expected to benefit the company.

Jefferies' Julien Dumoulin-Smith said NextEra is well positioned to benefit from the acceleration of renewables demand, but this growth will start materializing beyond the current 2024-27 plan.

BMO analyst James Thalacker said it expects the company’s management to reiterate its 6-8% LT growth outlook and 2024-2028 EPS.

“We expect investors' primary focus will be NEER's net backlog additions, initial FPL storm cost estimates, and updated views on the impact of the November election,” noted Thalacker.

Earlier in July, Florida-based NextEra posted second-quarter profit ahead of expectations, while revenue fell behind estimates.

Over the last two years, NEE has beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 50% of the time.

Seeking Alpha analysts and Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings are cautious and consider the stock a Hold. However, Wall Street analysts are bullish and rated it a Buy.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen four upward revisions, compared to three downward, while revenue estimates have been revised upwards twice versus two downward moves.

The stock has gained nearly 38% so far this year, compared to the about 23% rise in the broader S&P500 Index.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow fall as investors exercise caution amid earnings season
data.description:
The major market averages fell on Tuesday as investors grew cautious amid the earnings season, and as the U.S. presidential election nears.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.3%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.5%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.19%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) dipped 1 basis point to 4.00%.

Wall Street's major averages closed out the session mixed on Monday, as they wait for significant earnings reports to be released throughout the week.

"Yesterday, equities struggled, particularly as bond yields kept on rising," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Yesterday’s pontification of Federal Reserve speakers created a stir in bond markets. There were a range of views expressed, from “keep on cutting” to “more gradual cuts”. Markets, rendered skittish by Fed’s “data dependency” on undependable data, have raised yields, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"With two weeks to go today until the election, markets have started the week a bit more nervously than during the last six, where the S&P 500 has gone up each week for only the second time since the pandemic," Reid added.

On the earnings front, General Electric (GE), General Motors (GM), and Verizon Communications (VZ), among others, are expected to report before the bell on Tuesday.

data.title: Pfizer has “significantly overpaid” for recent acquisitions: Starboard Value
data.description:
Investment firm Starboard Value, which has launched an activist campaign against Pfizer (NYSE:PFE), upped the ante on the New York-based pharma giant on Tuesday, noting that the company has "significantly overpaid" for its acquisitions since 2022.

Presenting at the 2024 Active-Passive Investor Summit, Jeffrey Smith, CEO of Starboard Value, explained his activist stance on Pfizer (NYSE:PFE), a company credited with introducing the world's first COVID-19 vaccine in partnership with Germany's BioNTech (BNTX).

Smith noted that Pfizer (PFE) has lost $20B–$60B in market value since 2019 and argued that the company lost value due to a variety of issues ranging from capital allocation and R&D efficiency to errors in forecasting and budgeting.

His 74-page presentation detailed Pfizer's (PFE) recent setbacks, including a trial failure for the company's oral obesity candidate danuglipron in December and the recent market recall of sickle cell disease therapy Oxbryta.

A drug projected to have over $3B peak sales potential, Oxbryta was added to Pfizer's (PFE) portfolio following its $5B acquisition of Global Blood Therapeutics in 2022.

"For large pharma companies, like many other companies, it is critical to have the discipline to achieve the right return on investment," Smith said.

"We believe the Board needs to actively hold management accountable for earning appropriate returns on R&D and M&A moving forward."
